finding_results.notes: Revised the aftermath dialogue exchange (lines 365-387) to break Vel's three mini-speeches into movement-driven turns with concurrent physical action. (1) The body-first lecture ('Your feet. Your hands. Your head if you still have one') became compressed task enumeration ('Feet first. Hands. Whatever doesn't cost you levels.') broken by a treeline check. (2) The three-beat aphorism ('You don't have calibration. You have access. Those are not the same thing.') was replaced with specific field observation ('You aimed for one and spent fifteen. That's not something you fix by trying harder next time.') delivered while she scans and moves. (3) The quotable appetite diagnosis ('It isn't. It's appetite.') was replaced with physical observation and a practical directive ('your hands are still shaking. Keep them off the Ledger.') delivered while starting downhill. The closing 'Then until you learn, you don't feed it' (metaphor completing a crafted callback) became the blunter 'Then you don't use it' delivered without stopping. Removed narrator commentary that admired the dialogue ('The word hit harder than the binding cloth had'). Pre-fight Vel dialogue (current lines 71-87) was already roughened from the snapshot — no further changes needed there.

summary: Single finding addressed: roughened Vel's aftermath dialogue from polished thematic diagnosis into movement-driven field speech. Three multi-sentence lectures broken by concurrent physical action (treeline checks, scanning, adjusting pack, starting downhill). One corrective beat now shows pressure through a cutoff ('I know.' cutting Kem's excuse short) and two use purely functional wording instead of standalone quotable lines. No changes to pre-fight dialogue, combat narration, or chapter architecture. Heading contract, continuity, and all must-land beats preserved.
